objectives: Use ojalá + present subjunctive for hopes
Use ojalá + imperfect subjunctive for unlikely wishes
Form si + imperfect subjunctive + conditional for hypothetical scenarios
Express regret and wishes about the past

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Beatriz
dialogue_blocks.text: Ojalá que apruebe el examen mañana. Estudié muchísimo.
dialogue_blocks.translation: I hope I pass the exam tomorrow. I studied so much.

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Ernesto
dialogue_blocks.text: Ojalá pudieras calmarte. Te veo muy nerviosa.
dialogue_blocks.translation: I wish you could calm down. You look very nervous.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [oxaˈla puˈðjeɾas kalˈmaɾte ‖ te ˈβeo muj neɾˈβjosa]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Beatriz
dialogue_blocks.text: Si tuviera más tiempo, habría estudiado más. Pero empecé tarde.
dialogue_blocks.translation: If I had had more time, I would have studied more. But I started late.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [si tuˈβjeɾa ˈmas ˈtjempo ‖ aˈβɾia estuˈðjaðo ˈmas]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Ernesto
dialogue_blocks.text: Si yo fuera tú, haría un repaso rápido esta noche y dormiría bien.
dialogue_blocks.translation: If I were you, I'd do a quick review tonight and sleep well.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [si jo ˈfweɾa ˈtu ‖ aˈɾia un reˈpaso ˈrapiðo ˈesta ˈnoʧe]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Beatriz
dialogue_blocks.text: Ojalá que tengas razón. Si suspendiera, tendría que repetir el curso.
dialogue_blocks.translation: I hope you're right. If I failed, I would have to repeat the course.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [oxaˈla ke ˈteŋɡas raˈθon ‖ si suspenˈðjeɾa ‖ tenˈðɾia ke repeˈtiɾ el ˈkuɾso]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Ernesto
dialogue_blocks.text: No pienses en eso. Si me preguntaras, diría que estás muy preparada.
dialogue_blocks.translation: Don't think about that. If you asked me, I'd say you're very prepared.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [no ˈpjenses en ˈeso ‖ si me pɾeɣunˈtaɾas ‖ diˈɾia ke esˈtas muj pɾepaˈɾaða]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Beatriz
dialogue_blocks.text: Ojalá fuera tan segura de mí misma como tú pareces serlo.
dialogue_blocks.translation: I wish I were as self-assured as you seem to be.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [oxaˈla ˈfweɾa tan seˈɣuɾa ðe ˈmi ˈmisma ˈkomo ˈtu paˈɾeθes seɾlo]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Ernesto
dialogue_blocks.text: ¡Vas a salir bien! Y si no, ya verás — la próxima vez será diferente.
dialogue_blocks.translation: You'll do well! And if not, you'll see — next time will be different.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [ˈβas a saˈliɾ ˈβjen ‖ i si ˈno ‖ ja βeˈɾas ‖ la ˈpɾoksima ˈβeθ seˈɾa ðifeˈɾente]

srs_flashcards.back: I hope... (possible): Ojalá llegue a tiempo.
I wish... (unlikely/impossible): Ojalá tuviera más dinero.
If I had time, I would... (present hypothetical)
I sure hope so! (affirmative wish response)
I would like to... (polite/hypothetical wish, imperfect subj of querer)

exercises.instruction: Escoge la forma correcta para expresar un deseo hipotético
exercises.question: You wish you could fly — it's clearly not possible right now. Which is correct?

feedback.correct: ¡Correcto! 'Ojalá pudiera' (imperfect subjunctive) = impossible or very unlikely wish. 'Ojalá pueda' would imply it might actually happen.
feedback.incorrect: 'Ojalá pudiera volar' (imperfect subjunctive) is correct for impossible wishes. 'Ojalá pueda' (present subjunctive) is for possible wishes.

exercises.instruction: Une la estructura hipotética con su significado

pairs.english: I wish I had more vacation. (impossible/unlikely)

pairs.spanish: Si viviera cerca, te visitaría más.
pairs.english: If I lived nearby, I'd visit you more. (hypothetical)

pairs.spanish: Si hubiera sabido, no lo habría comprado.
pairs.english: If I had known, I wouldn't have bought it. (past regret)

exercises.instruction: Completa con la forma correcta del subjuntivo o condicional

dialogue.speaker: Paz
dialogue.text: Ojalá _____ mañana. Me lo merezco después de tanto trabajo. (hacer buen tiempo — hacer)

dialogue.speaker: Gus
dialogue.text: ¡Ojalá! Si _____ buen tiempo, podríamos ir a la playa. (hacer — hypothetical)

dialogue.speaker: Paz
dialogue.text: Quisiera _____ todo y quedarme ahí una semana. (olvidar — yo)

dialogue.speaker: Gus
dialogue.text: Si _____ más días libres, lo haríamos. (tener — nosotros)

speaking_rubric.title: Deseos e hipótesis (Wishes and hypotheticals)
speaking_rubric.scenario: Share your wishes and hypotheticals with a friend — what you hope will happen (ojalá + present subj), what you wish were true (ojalá + imperfect subj), and what you'd do in a hypothetical scenario (si + imperfect subj + conditional).
speaking_rubric.prompt: Expresa deseos reales, deseos imposibles y situaciones hipotéticas usando ojalá y si.

scoring_criteria.description: Correct subjunctive forms with ojalá and si clauses

sample_response.text: Ojalá que apruebe mis exámenes finales esta semana. Ojalá pudiera tomar un descanso largo después — lo necesito. Si tuviera un mes libre, viajaría por Sudamérica sin plan fijo.
sample_response.ipa: [oxaˈla ke apˈɾweβe mis ekˈsamenes ˈfinales ‖ oxaˈla puˈðjeɾa toˈmaɾ un desˈkanse laɾɣo]

cultural_notes.formality: 'Ojalá' is one of the most emotionally rich words in Spanish — it carries hope, longing, and resignation depending on context. Mexicans use it constantly in everyday speech, far beyond textbook usage.
cultural_notes.gestures: Clasping hands briefly while saying 'ojalá' is a natural, almost prayer-like gesture in Mexican culture — reflecting the word's Arabic/religious origin.
cultural_notes.regional_variations: 'Ojalá' is universal across all Spanish dialects. Puerto Ricans often add 'así sea' (so may it be) after ojalá for emphasis. Mexicans may say '¡Ojalá y!' (an informal intensifier) before the subjunctive.
